findings.problem: The chapter already turns Daro's claim into public doctrine on the porch and shows the yard splitting around it, but the late-night house scene largely asks the crowd to make the same ideological choice again. The deadline revelation is strong, yet too much of the late-room material replays an argument whose outcome the chapter has already established, which softens the launch beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the deadline material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:295 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:303, but re-engineer the late-room section around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:321, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:347, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:395 so it produces a new form of consequence instead of another crowd reconversion. Options: make the deadline force a concrete sacrifice, an irreversible logistical split, or a public abandonment that the porch scene could not already supply.
findings.acceptance_test: After the deadline is stated at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:295 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:303, the revised late-room scene must introduce at least one materially new conflict beat: a named loss, a forced crew choice, or an irreversible tactical concession visible on the page. If the room mainly restates heir doctrine and chooses Daro again in near-identical terms, fail.

findings.problem: When the house turns Daro into a chosen body, the prose identifies the fusion of devotion, power, and desire but then retreats into euphemism: 'Some of it was power. Some of it was plain body' and 'the heat of attention settling low in him.' In a novel whose style bible explicitly asks for the concrete collision of sanctity and lust, this is sanitization drift at the exact point where the bodily cost of belief should become specific and uncomfortable.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the petition sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:241 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:243 so the eel-oil touch and Daro's response are rendered with exact bodily and social consequence. Keep the public setting, but replace the abstract summary with what the contact does to his throat, breath, posture, arousal, shame, or self-control, and show what the waiting room can or cannot read from him.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised version of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:241 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:243, the passage must include at least one precise bodily response in Daro and one observable social consequence of his letting the touch linger. Abstract labels such as 'power,' 'plain body,' or generalized 'heat' without a concrete manifestation do not pass.

findings.problem: The chapter keeps returning to the same 'as if' comparison scaffold for emphasis. Because it appears across all three scenes, the device stops feeling chosen and starts sounding automatic, flattening otherwise strong images into a repeated sentence habit.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the recurring comparison frames at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:15, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:47, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:117, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:125, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:139, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:165, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:195, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:241, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:337, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:365, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:389, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:401. Preserve the pressure in those beats, but vary the syntax by using direct statement, different metaphor structures, or sharper concrete description instead of the same 'as if' hinge.
findings.acceptance_test: Across the cited lines, no more than two 'as if' comparison frames remain in the revised chapter, and each replacement must still deliver a concrete image or pressure effect that is readable on first pass.

summary: Booker and dialogue pass: the chapter cleanly publicizes the schism, fixes the flood-crest deadline, and keeps Daro, Riva, Lale, Tovin, Bato, and Nami distinct in register and pressure. It still fails on craft and prose because the late-night room scene partially replays an already-won public choice, Daro's petition sequence sanitizes the sanctity-lust contamination the style bible calls for, and the prose leans too often on the same 'as if' comparison scaffold.
